Background
The U Rock Math tutoring program was devised to support elementary and middle school students in mathematics. Founder, Dr. Daryl Rock is a former math teacher, high school principal, and superintendent. When he left his principalship at Benjamin Banneker Academy, that school had the highest graduation rate for African American students in New York State. When students get individualized instruction and consistent time to practice in a structured, mutually respectful, learning environment, they can make substantial progress in mathematics. These are the principles that guide U Rock Math.
Overview
At each school for 100 days, 5-10 high school juniors or seniors will be hired to support 30-60 elementary or middle school students in grades 3-8. The principal will determine which students will be involved. Two 55 minute sessions will be held after school from 3-5pm Tuesday to Fridays. Tutoring will not be conducted on Mondays, but the session will be used as the prep day for tutors to prepare materials for the week and meet with teachers. Each tutor will be responsible for 3-4 students per session, all from the same class. Tutors will be recruited and interviewed beforehand and will be hired based on mathematical ability, availability, and personality. Many are bilingual. During each session, a teacher–in charge will be present, along with a college coordinator. They will supervise the program and tutor students in small groups. The college coordinator will also tutor students in small groups in addition to other duties.

S & S Health and Wellness is a mobile, community-centered wellness company dedicated to fostering holistic health and empowerment across New York City and surrounding areas. As a Black-owned, family-operated business based in the Bronx, we are proud to serve diverse communities through accessible wellness programs, trauma informed services, and natural self-care products. Our mission is to bridge the wellness gap by offering culturally relevant practices that support the whole person—mind, body, and spirit.

We specialize in providing customized, scalable wellness programming to schools, early childhood centers, public agencies, afterschool programs, housing developments, senior centers, and community-based organizations. With a unique blend of yoga, mindfulness, music and movement (such as Zumbini®), holistic education, and natural wellness products, like smoothies and wellness drinks, our work supports social-emotional learning (SEL), mental health, emotional regulation, and community well-being.

Our offerings include:
	•	Children’s Yoga & Mindfulness: Evidence-based, age-appropriate classes for preschoolers, school-age children, 
teens and college students that promote focus, self-regulation, movement, and calm.
	•	Family and Community Wellness Events: Interactive wellness workshops, movement circles, sound baths, and outdoor yoga sessions for families and caregivers.
	•	Staff Wellness for Schools & Agencies: Self-care sessions, restorative yoga, and mindfulness trainings for educators, social workers, and staff to reduce burnout and increase resilience.
	•	Holistic Products: Our signature line of handmade, small-batch products—including magnesium sprays, essential oil inhalers, and soothing balms—support physical and emotional wellness and are often incorporated into our workshops and offerings.
	•	Music & Movement for Early Childhood: Zumbini® for Schools and community programs, where we combine music, dance, and play to support early learning and bonding in a joyful, high-energy format.
	•	Seasonal and Culturally Relevant Events: We also organize and lead events like community healing circles, yoga in the park, and holistic pop-ups that reflect the needs and values of the communities we serve.

business_name: Speak Easy Speech Services, SLP, P.C.
description:
Speak Easy Speech Services SLP P.C. dba World Class Speech Services was founded in July 2002. It is a certified Minority/Women Business Enterprise for NY state and NY city. The president and owner is Dawn Cotter-Jenkins (MA, CCC-SLP; MSHI). World Class Speech Services is a multifaceted company that provides professional solutions to improve speech and communication personal and technology needs for individuals and organizations.

1.      How much time has been wasted in miscommunication that creates disgruntled workers or customers? Losing good people and revenue, or adding expenses to readvertise to refill the position or find new customers.
2.     How much better can the people in your organization work with fewer bottlenecks in interpersonal communication? Intervening in disputes and calling in HR to mediate.
3.     What has it cost your company in wasted workflow hours to go to meeting after meeting to make decisions and strategize? What should have been done in three meetings, ends up taking six.

Speech and communication in the workplace can directly or indirectly affect the productivity and success of your company. If your employees are interacting with customers or clients, it is important for them to carry the company messages in a clear and direct manner. If they are relaying information to each other, instructions and follow-up need to be effective and direct. Employees may come from a diverse range of departments and need to both listen and speak for maximum effectiveness and timeliness, minimizing going back and forth for clarification. Leaders, employees, and clients benefit from improved communication. Whether it’s for sharing information, sales, or leadership, ongoing skills development is often needed.  

Communication challenges in many workplaces can be in:
·        Speech clarity
·        Message content
·        Listening skills
·        Interpersonal communication styles

Avoiding these challenges can save time and increase productivity and outcomes. When coworkers know how to improve their speech, voice, and communication they can express their needs and share information much more efficiently. Learning techniques and strategies for better communication, as an individual or team, can improve confidence and build collaboration. Doing so cuts down on miscommunications, allows for better feedback and follow-up, and saves time in workflow.

What makes World Class Speech Services different?

World Class Speech Services is able to address a variety of goals in all of these areas due to specialized training and decades of experience. The founder and president, Dawn Cotter-Jenkins, has been teaching and training adults since 2003 in different colleges and organizations on workplace communication, voice and diction, accent modification, and public speaking, to name a few courses.
She has worked as a:
·        TEDx Speaker Coach
·        Certified speaker, trainer and coach in the John Maxwell Leadership Program
·        University/college adjunct professor
·        Licensed and certified speech-language pathologist (SLP)

Dawn Cotter-Jenkins, of World Class Speech Services, has expanded her scope of work to include Diversity, Equity, Inclusion and Accessibility (DEI&A) strategies and telepractice training and development over the past 5 years.
·        Lead the development of the NY State Licensing Board Telepractice Guidelines
·        Created a curriculum for an elective course and an internship at Adelphi University
·        Certified in Diversity, Equity and Inclusion in the Workplace from the University of South Florida  

From entry positions to the C-Suite, as a speaker, instructor, coach, trainer, and consultant, I work with many facets of workforce communication. Not only do I bring in evidence-based and innovative methods, I co-create curriculum to directly address your company's needs. Using a needs assessment, pre-training questionnaires, and interviews, World Class Speech Services will determine priorities and hone the program goals to your company’s specific high priority needs for individuals or groups. I analyze and provide strategic guidance with ongoing feedback to adress systemic and organizational needs to take you from good to great.  

World Class Speech Services is a multi-specialty company. Our unique qualifications, as compared to other speech and communication coaches, are deep and extensive layers of professional certifications and licenses in speech-language-communication assessment and intervention. Additional trainings as a speaker-trainer-coach with the John Maxwell Leadership Program, being a TEDx speaker coach, and certification and practice in Diversity, Equity and Inclusion makes me highly qualified to recognize, listen and address your needs. 

To learn more, see my website for testimonials from previous clients. Contact me today to set up a meeting to discuss your company's unique goals.

At STRUT, we pride ourselves on being more than just a modeling agency; we're a beacon for those committed to excellence and inclusivity. With a commitment to community and social impact, the STRUT-Esteem program was born in 2014 under the auspices of STRUT Models. This program leverages the foundational principles of modeling to boost self-esteem among young people. This innovative program has been implemented in NYC public schools and even extended to police departments, addressing the critical issue of low self-esteem that often plagues youth. By focusing on self-value and worth, the program aims to mitigate the negative effects of peer pressure, low academic performance, violence, and other detrimental behaviors. Through its curriculum, STRUT-Esteem not only enhances personal confidence but also fosters a sense of empowerment and resilience in participants.

Beyond its primary goal of improving self-esteem, the STRUT-Esteem program offers young people an introduction to the modeling industry, highlighting various career paths available within it. This exposure broadens their horizons and ignites their inner creativity, encouraging them to envision and pursue diverse professional opportunities. The program's activities and teachings enable young individuals to develop crucial life skills that are beneficial both academically and socially. By nurturing their talents and encouraging personal growth, STRUT Esteem helps participants flourish into their best selves, preparing them for future success and well-being.

As a Black-owned and female-driven establishment, diversity and inclusion are at the core of everything we do. As a model or client of STRUT, you're not just changing the face of fashion; you're transforming its very essence. Uncle Yankey's Pepper Sauce was created in 2021 in the midst of the Covid pandemic and although it was very daunting at first, I went along with my dream of bringing a piece of my homeland from Trinidad and Tobago to the US to share with others.
I set out to recreate Uncle Yankey’s pepper sauce from my profound memories of home by staying true to my culture by incorporating fresh fruits, fresh seasonings with only the best peppers available while developing my own niche. 
Uncle Yankey’s started as a home based business in Brooklyn where it’s now being manufactured in Cook Collective Kitchen in Brooklyn, NY.
No other companies was offering hot sauce in quite this way where we took exotic tropical fruits like pineapple, passion fruit, papaya, mango along with healthy vegetables like beets and bell peppers, mixed in with fresh seasonings and raw peppers to create a flavorful and savory hot sauce with an intense heat that will leave your palate bursting with great robust flavors that could only be found in the Caribbean. 
When I sell Uncle Yankey’s pepper sauce to my customers I just don’t want to make a sale. I want them to enjoy the experience of seeing how colorful each blends looks, smell the fragrant aroma and taste each and flavorful ingredients it’s made with so they can develop a love for my pepper sauce just like I do and become a regular where my pepper sauce will become a staple item in their home. 
As of today Uncle Yankey’s has 12 great bold flavors for everyone’s palate, pineapple, grapefruit, strawberry, tropical, virgin tropical, scorpion mango, exotic grapes, healthy beets, avocado and banana pepper sauce, avocado and homemade pepper sauce.

description: Vibrant Women Wellness Counseling works to strengthen women's resilience amidst life challenges by using a holistic approach to address areas of mental, physical, and spiritual health. Vibrant Women Wellness Counseling workshops include: How to Support Yourself and Others with Anxiety, First Time Parenting Strategies, Healthy Attachment in Relationships, Healing through Art, Healthy Communication in Relationships, How to Positively Cope with Stress, How to Support Mental Health Concerns in the Workplace, How to Support Mental Health with Family and Friends, The Mind-body Connection 4 part Series: 1) trauma informed yoga as a tool for well-being, 2) the nervous system, 3) hormones and our mood, and 4) benefits of exercise. Other workshop topics include Nutrition Basics, Procrastination: The Whys & How to Move Forward, Promoting Family Health, Self-care, Stress Management for High Burnout Professions, Today's Family: Challenges and Changes, Understanding Mental Health, and Understanding Personality Types. There are also community engagement events which focus on developing social support for clientele.

Vibrnt Seeds is a culturally grounded, education-driven initiative rooted in the belief that every child deserves access to hands-on learning, fresh food, and opportunities to connect with the natural world. Blooming from the urgent needs revealed during the COVID-19 pandemic, Vibrnt Seeds began as a community response to support essential workers and their families through engaging, garden-based education and food literacy programming. What started as resource kits and virtual lessons has now grown into a dynamic platform that brings urban agriculture, nutrition, and sustainability to life inside classrooms, afterschool programs, and community spaces across New York City.

At its core, Vibrnt Seeds helps young people—and the adults who guide them—rediscover the joy and power of growing food, no matter their background or living situation. Through vibrant partnerships with schools, community centers, shelters, and public housing organizations, Vibrnt Seeds installs edible garden kits, facilitates experiential workshops, and provides technical support to educators, site directors, and caregivers. The program is committed to making growing food a joyful, accessible experience—whether it’s through a hydroponic tower in a cafeteria corner, tabletop gardens in a second-grade classroom, or windowsill herb kits that children proudly take home to their families.

One of the signature features of Vibrnt Seeds is its Tabletop Garden Series, where students engage in hands-on activities that teach science, math, and storytelling through the act of growing. Each child receives their own edible herb kit, designed for indoor growing on a windowsill, which helps extend learning from school into the home. These kits aren’t just plants—they’re a pathway to understanding food systems, building healthy routines, and fostering family connection. The installations are paired with curated lesson plans and simple instructions so that educators—regardless of gardening experience—feel confident leading sessions.

Beyond the classroom, Vibrnt Seeds brings its educational mission into the broader community. The program hosts seasonal events, parent workshops, pop-up plant carts, and garden clubs that make it easy for entire families to participate in growing, cooking, and learning together. Whether working alongside school wellness councils, community-based organizations, or city agencies, Vibrnt Seeds shows up as both a collaborator and a cultivator—helping communities turn small spaces into impactful growing environments that support wellness, creativity, and food access.

Vibrnt Seeds also addresses deeper systemic challenges, such as food insecurity and environmental justice, through programs that connect students to local food sources and green career pathways. Youth are introduced to farming technologies, cultural planting traditions, and even animal husbandry as part of a broader ecosystem of sustainability education. All activities are designed to be joyful, inclusive, and reflective of the communities they serve—particularly Black and Brown youth in historically under-resourced neighborhoods.

What makes Vibrnt Seeds unique is its ability to bridge education with action. The program blends curriculum-aligned learning with real-world experiences that nurture a child’s sense of agency and belonging. At a time when school communities are seeking meaningful ways to support student mental health, family engagement, and culturally responsive learning, Vibrnt Seeds offers a model that is tactile, inspiring, and deeply rooted in care.

Agencies and partners who work with Vibrnt Seeds can expect a hands-on, collaborative process that meets their needs while uplifting the voices of their students and families. Whether you're looking to install a long-term edible garden, run a short-term workshop series, or incorporate youth-friendly wellness initiatives into your programming, Vibrnt Seeds brings passion, expertise, and a deep love for community to every project.

In a city where concrete often replaces soil and fast food overshadows fresh produce, Vibrnt Seeds offers something radical: a way for children and families to grow something real—together.
